title: Bridge WBTC - Open App
meta_description: bridge wbtc across supported networks with wallet-ready routes, quote review, and a direct handoff to the official bridge app.
hero_h2: Move Wrapped Bitcoin to the network where you need it
hero_lead: bridge wbtc opens a focused route for moving Wrapped Bitcoin between supported networks, checking the destination chain, quote, approval, and app domain before you sign.
what_is: Jumper is a multichain routing app for swaps and cross-chain transfers, while WBTC is the wrapped Bitcoin token described on the WBTC official site. Use the app to select WBTC, compare the route shown, and continue only on the verified domain.

how.d: Use the official domain, connect the wallet that holds WBTC, and confirm the site shown in your wallet prompt.
Select WBTC as the asset, set the source network, then pick a destination network available in the app.
Check the bridge provider, estimated output, gas needs, approval request, and recipient address before signing.
Approve only the required token spend, submit the transfer, and follow status in the app or on the relevant explorer.

points.p: The route screen should keep asset, source chain, destination chain, quote, and recipient visible before execution.
When checking the Ethereum asset, compare it with the WBTC token contract before adding or approving tokens.
Primary calls to action should resolve to jumper.xyz, and wallet prompts should match the same domain context.

faq.a: It means moving Wrapped Bitcoin from one supported blockchain network to another through a bridge or routing app while keeping the asset selected as WBTC where available.

faq.q: Do I need native BTC for this flow?
faq.a: No. If you already hold WBTC, the app uses WBTC as the asset; you still need the source chain's native gas token and may need destination gas after arrival.

faq.q: Can I send WBTC to any chain?
faq.a: No. Use only the source and destination networks shown as available in the official app, because token variants and bridge support can differ by chain.

faq.q: Why is an approval shown before the transfer?
faq.a: WBTC is an ERC-20 token, so your wallet may ask you to approve a specific spend before the bridge or router can move the amount you choose.

faq.q: How do I verify the destination?
faq.a: Check the app domain, selected networks, recipient address, token symbol, contract details, and transaction status on the relevant explorer before and after signing.

footer_note: This page is not the official app; verify jumper.xyz before connecting a wallet; educational content only, not financial advice.
